rulings.Case Title: JASON GILL VS. OFFICE OF SHORT TERM RENTALS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2021 line 1. RESPONDENTS OFFICE OF SHORT TERM RENTALS AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION Hearing Required to address whether petitioner can allege in good faith that the third-floor bedroom was legal at all relevant times. If Petitioner contends that such amendment can be made, Petitioner/counsel to submit a declaration no later than 9:00 a.m. on September 23, 2021 with proposed amendment (only paragraphs to be added/amended). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion Of Defendant Stonstown Shopping Center Lp For Costs And Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF ANGELA LEUNG'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING MOTION OF DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER LP FOR COSTS AND ATTORNEYS FEES is denied. Although Ms. Leung fails to show any new facts, law or circumstances as required by CCP 1008, the court, on its own motion per its inherent authority, once again considered whether paragraph 20.2 of the parties' lease provided for an award of fees to SSC. The court remains of the view that paragraph 20.2 is a prevailing party fees provision that is applicable to the circumstances of this case. SSC's request for supplemental fees is granted in part. SSC is awarded $490 in fees based on two hours of its counsel's time. Given the simplicity of this motion, the reasonable amount of time needed to oppose the motion does not exceed two hours. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice And Motion Requesting Ccp 1008 (B) Reconsideration Of Denial Of Plaintiffs April 02, 2021 Request For April Hearings Pause Due To Illness And Ada Requests, Allowing Order For Attorneys Fees And Costs Heard April 23 2021 And June 02, 2021 Vexatious Litigant With New Information
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON'S NOTICE AND MOTION REQUESTING CCP 1008(B) RECONSIDERATION OF DENIAL OF PLAINTIFF'S APRIL 2, 2021 REQUEST FOR APRIL HEARINGS PAUSE DUE TO ILLNESS AND ADA REQUESTS, ALLOWING ORDER FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS HEARD APRIL 23, 2021 AND JUNE 2, 2021 VEXATIOUS LITIGANT WITH NEW INFORMATION is DENIED. Request for reconsideration is untimely and made after defendant Ma's dismissal. Furthermore, the was no "denial" of April 2, 2021 request. Said request appears in the Register of Action as an unnoticed (for motion or hearing) letter to the Court seeking to continue Motion for Summary Judgment. Motion for Summary Judgment was never heard and was taken off calendar. Any reconsideration of the April 2, 2021 to continue the hearing would be futile. April 2, 2021 letter did not mention April 23, 2021 hearing and could not have mentioned June 2, 2021 hearing as it has not yet been on calendar as of April 2, 2021. Furthermore, on April 23, 2021 Defendant's Motion for fees was denied and Plaintiff is the prevailing party on the motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of & Motion Of Ccp 473(B)For Discretionary Relief Due To A Excusable Mistake, Surprise, Inadvertence To Vacate April 23, 2021 Order For Costs, Fees, And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON'S NOTICE OF & MOTION OF CCP 473(B) FOR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF DUE TO EXCUSABLE MISTAKE, SURPRISE, INADVERTENCE TO VACATE APRIL 23, 2021 ORDER FOR COSTS, FEES, AND SANCTIONS is DENIED. There is no April 23, 2021 Order for Costs, Fess and Sanctions on file. Register of Actions does not reflect any orders entered on that day. On April 23, 2021 the Court heard and DENIED Defendant Ma's Motion for Attorney's Fees and the prevailing party (here Plaintiff) was instructed to provide a proposed order for execution. It does not appear from the record that a proposed order was ever submitted by the Plaintiff. The Court further notes that this motion was not properly set for hearing either per CCP 1005 or per June 4, 2021 Order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
